channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Coca-Cola’s new holiday ad turned me off of AI-generated art completely
item.description: I am so over AI-generated art. It just needs to go away. Please. Like everyone, I was captivated by the tech as it emerged in 2022. As a novel technology, it was pretty mind-blowing.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: OneOdio Focus A5 Pro Headphones Review: Affordable Noise Cancellation and Hi-Res Audio
item.description: OneOdio's new Focus A5 Pro headphones offer impressive specs at an affordable price, with hybrid ANC, LDAC streaming, and 75 hours of battery life.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Social platform for US and UK military may have exposed over a million records
item.description: Armed forces members could be subject to identity theft attacks and extortion after exposed database was discovered.
item.dc:creator: benedict.collins@futurenet.com (Benedict Collins), TechRadar

item.title: Thai court dismisses activist's suit against Israeli spyware producer over lack of evidence
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A Thai court on Thursday threw out a lawsuit brought by a pro-democracy activist which alleged spyware produced by an Israeli tech firm had been used to hack his phone.
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: AI-powered dog robot sniffs out invasive fire ants
item.description: Fueled by artificial intelligence, CyberDog the robotic dog is designed specifically locate fire ant nests, which are endangering ecosystems.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FedEx CIO Rob Carter on building a world-class IT culture
item.description: Robert B. Carter is a visionary technology executive whose pioneering career and accomplishments have set the standard for IT leadership excellence.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: US regulators seek to break up Google, forcing Chrome sale as part of monopoly punishment
item.description: U.S. regulators want a federal judge to break up Google to prevent the company from continuing to squash competition through its dominant search engine after a court found it had maintained an abusive monopoly over the past decade.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Future of AI: Red Hat Partners with Microsoft for Cloud Deployment
item.description: Red Hat and Microsoft collaborate to make RHEL AI and JBoss EAP 8 available on Azure cloud platform, revolutionizing AI development and application modernization.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Comcast to spin off cable networks that were once the entertainment giant's star performers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Comcast is spinning off into a new company many of the cable television networks that were once at the entertainment giant's heart as consumers increasingly swap out their cable TV subscriptions for streaming platforms.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US gathers allies to talk AI safety as Trump's vow to undo Biden's AI policy overshadows their work
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has vowed to repeal President Joe Biden's signature artificial intelligence policy when he returns to the White House for a second term. What that actually means for the future of AI technology remains to be seen.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Uber announces ‘XXL’ rides. Here’s what to know about the new service
item.description: Uber Technologies launched “XXL” rides with extra trunk space on Wednesday, betting on increased demand stemming from Thanksgiving holiday travelers, as the ride-hailing company seeks to overcome a slowdown in its mainstay app-based taxi business.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Scammers are making thousands of dollars through blockchain typosquatting
item.description: The crypto revolution is in full force following Donald Trump’s re-election. But those looking to send and receive crypto will have to beware of so-called “typosquatting” scams, or risk losing thousands of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Whoever ends up owning Chrome, we could use a new browser war
item.description: Do you have a favorite web browser? I used to: It was called Flock, had an emphasis on social networking and sharing, and died in 2011. Nothing I’ve used since has warmed my heart quite so much. On Monday, Bloomberg’s Leah Nylen and Josh Sisco reported that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: US gathers allies to talk AI safety. Trump’s vow to undo Biden’s AI policy overshadows their work
item.description: Their agenda addresses topics such as how to better detect and combat a flood of AI-generated deepfakes fueling fraud, harmful impersonation and sexual abuse.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Chatbot's disturbing message
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft pitches AI 'agents' that can perform tasks on their own at Ignite 2024
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella told customers at a conference in Chicago on Tuesday that the company is teaching a new set of artificial intelligence tools how to "act on our behalf across our work and life.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A matter of time: 3 metrics that demonstrate the value of immersion cooling
item.description: Immersion cooling in the data center space gets a lot of positive attention for the formulations that enhance thermal performance, material compatibility, and durability.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: German police break up an environmental activists' camp near Tesla plant outside Berlin
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German police broke up a protest camp housing environmental activists in a forest near the Tesla electric car factory in Grünheide outside Berlin on Tuesday, citing violations of public safety and order.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How Trump’s appointment of Brendan Carr as FCC chairman will affect enterprises
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has announced Brendan Carr as the next head of the US Federal Communications Commission (FCC), the organization responsible for setting the regulations governing telecoms, broadband, satellite communications and broadcasting in the US.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Lawrence Livermore supercomputer is crowned world’s speediest
item.description: "El Capitan" takes top spot in global supercomputer race, helping tackle daunting challenges in national security and science.
item.dc:creator: Lisa M. Krieger, The Mercury News

item.title: Helpful hack: Customize your home screen by adding widgets on your iPhone
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson shows you how to add, customize and manage widgets for quick access to apps and info from your iPhone home screen.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Spin 3.0 supports polyglot development using Wasm components
item.description: Fermyon has released Spin 3.0, a major update to its open source developer tool for building serverless WebAssembly applications. The new release introduces polyglot programming to ease development. The update to Fermyon’s Spin framework was introduced November 11.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Millions in high speed internet grants awarded in rural New York
item.description: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer and Gov. Kathy Hochul announced the funds for open-access fiber, wireless hubs.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation
